data.title: Umbra's privacy policy disables the front end to prevent attackers from transferring stolen Kelp-related funds.
data.description: Umbra, a privacy protocol provider, has shut down its hosted front-end website to prevent attackers from using the protocol to transfer funds stolen in a recent security incident. Umbra stated that approximately $800,000 was transferred through its protocol, but the protocol was only used to conceal the recipient's identity; the transactions are still traceable on-chain. This action comes after the attack on the Kelp protocol, which resulted in losses exceeding $280 million. Umbra said it will restore front-end services once it confirms that this will not affect asset recovery efforts, but it cannot prevent users from continuing to use the protocol via smart contracts or self-hosted front-ends. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Umbra Temporarily Shuts Down Front End Amid Stolen Funds Movement
data.description: Umbra has placed its hosted front end into maintenance mode following the movement of approximately $800,000 in stolen funds through its privacy protocol. According to NS3.AI, the company stated that the site will be restored once reopening does not hinder recovery efforts. This decision follows the recent $280 million Kelp exploit, which reports suggest involved attempts to transfer funds from Ether to Bitcoin via Umbra.

data.title: Sullivan & Cromwell Apologizes for AI-Induced Errors in Court Filing
data.description: Wall Street law firm Sullivan & Cromwell has issued an apology to a federal judge following an emergency court filing that included approximately 40 incorrect citations and other errors attributed to AI hallucinations. According to NS3.AI, Andrew Dietderich, a representative of the firm, acknowledged that the firm's citation review policies were not adhered to in this instance. Sullivan & Cromwell is currently undertaking an internal investigation to address the issue and prevent future occurrences.

data.title: Middle East Ceasefire Extension Causes Market Uncertainty; U.S. Stocks Decline
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). The extension of the ceasefire in the Middle East has led to market unease, with the three major U.S. stock indices ending a 13-day rally by closing lower. Meanwhile, crude oil prices surged past $100, while gold prices fell. Expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut have cooled.

In Asia, Japan's Nikkei and South Korea's KOSPI reached new highs. Bitcoin surpassed $77,000, with analysts divided on its future trajectory, citing risks of both rebound and correction.

The Russell 2000 index broke through a five-year resistance level, suggesting that liquidity injections might support risk assets. However, the correlation with altcoins has turned negative.

data.title: Tesla to Release Q1 Earnings as U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Deadline Approaches
data.description: The deadline for the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement is set to expire on the morning of April 23. According to PANews, Tesla is scheduled to release its first-quarter earnings report later that day. Meanwhile, the historical correlation between the Russell 2000 index and altcoins has turned negative for the first time.

data.title: UK Inflation Rises Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: UK inflation rose from 3.0% in February to 3.3% in March, according to data released on Wednesday. According to Jin10, this increase suggests that the Middle East conflict is beginning to impact prices. Before the US and Israel took action against Iran, the Bank of England indicated that UK inflation might approach its 2% target level in April. However, last month, the Bank of England significantly raised its inflation forecast due to an energy price shock, projecting inflation to rise to around 3.5% by mid-2026. The International Monetary Fund predicted last week that UK inflation would peak at 4% in the coming months. Despite these projections, most Bank of England policymakers believe it is too early to determine how the overall rise in inflation will affect underlying price pressures in the economy. The current weak job market may make it difficult for workers to demand higher wages or for businesses to pass on increased costs to consumers.

data.title: Semiconductor Index Rises for 14 Consecutive Sessions, Hedge Funds Increase Tech Exposure
data.description:
The Semiconductor Index (SOX) has experienced a significant rise, marking 14 consecutive trading sessions of gains and achieving a 34.4% increase during this period. According to NS3.AI, this upward trend reflects growing investor confidence in the semiconductor sector.

Additionally, hedge funds have shown a strong preference for global technology stocks, purchasing more in this sector than any other last week. Their gross and net exposure to technology stocks has increased to 28.3% and 34.0%, respectively. This shift indicates a strategic move by hedge funds to capitalize on the robust performance of the technology sector.

data.title: WOJAK's market capitalization briefly broke through to a new high, surging over 100% intraday.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of WOJAK, the Meme coin on the Ethereum blockchain, briefly broke through to a new high, currently trading at $43.19 million, with a daily increase of over 100%. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coins is highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: CHIP Experiences Significant Price Surge
data.description: CHIP's price surged significantly, breaking past $0.08 before the market opened and is currently reported at $0.077. According to Odaily, the cryptocurrency has seen an increase of over 150% within the day.

data.title: UK Core CPI Annual Rate for March Slightly Below Expectations
data.description: The UK's core Consumer Price Index (CPI) annual rate for March was reported at 3.1%, according to Jin10. This figure is slightly below the anticipated 3.2% and matches the previous month's rate of 3.20%. The core CPI excludes volatile items such as food and energy, providing a clearer view of underlying inflation trends. This data is closely watched by policymakers and investors as it can influence monetary policy decisions.

data.title: UK Retail Price Index Annual Rate Rises to 4.1% in March
data.description: The UK's Retail Price Index (RPI) annual rate increased to 4.1% in March, surpassing the expected 4% and the previous value of 3.60%. According to Jin10, this rise in the RPI indicates a higher-than-anticipated inflation rate, which could have implications for consumer spending and economic policy decisions in the UK.

data.title: UK March Unadjusted Output PPI Annual Rate Exceeds Expectations
data.description: The UK's March unadjusted Output Producer Price Index (PPI) annual rate was reported at 2.6%, surpassing the anticipated 2.4%. According to Jin10, the previous value was revised from 1.70% to 1.8%.

data.title: River Concludes S4 Season with Over 250,000 Participants
data.description: River, a blockchain-based stablecoin system, has announced the conclusion of its S4 season, which attracted over 250,000 users. According to Foresight News, the season saw the distribution of 200 million River points, with staking volumes increasing from zero to approximately $30 million worth of RIVER tokens. Details regarding the claim date and final amount for S4 will be released later. The S5 season has now commenced.

data.title: Thailand Adjusts State Spending Amid Oil Shock to Maintain Deficit Target
data.description: Thailand is reorganizing its state spending to mitigate the effects of an oil shock while ensuring that its deficit target remains unchanged. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's efforts to balance fiscal policies in response to rising oil prices. The move aims to stabilize the economy without altering the planned budget deficit, reflecting a strategic approach to economic management in challenging times.

data.title: Iranian media have confirmed receiving "some signals" that the United States is preparing to lift the blockade.
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim News Agency, an Iranian envoy stated that Iran is prepared to negotiate once the United States lifts its naval blockade. The Iranian state media released a video with a caption stating that Iran's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Amir Saeed Illavani, said Tehran is prepared to negotiate immediately after the US ends its naval blockade, emphasizing that Washington must first cease its "violations of the ceasefire agreement." Following the report, the market reacted swiftly, with gold and silver prices rising briefly, while the US dollar and crude oil prices fell. Previously, the Associated Press, RIA Novosti, and other media outlets had reported on the ambassador's remarks; this latest report comes from an Iranian state media outlet linked to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. (Jinshi)

data.title: MGBX will launch spot trading for USD.AI (CHIP) and Genius (GENIUS).
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will launch spot trading of USD.AI (CHIP) and Genius (GENIUS) on April 22, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Deposits will open at 16:00 (SGT) on April 22, 2026; trading will open at 18:00 (SGT) on April 22, 2026; withdrawals will open at 19:00 (SGT) on April 22, 2026.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,400 USDT with a 3.71% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 22, 2026, 05:32 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,400 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,401.129883 USDT, with a narrowed 3.71% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitcoin Rises as U.S. President Trump Extends Ceasefire with Iran
data.description:
Bitcoin experienced a notable increase alongside equities on Wednesday following an announcement by U.S. President Donald Trump regarding the extension of a ceasefire with Iran. According to Bloomberg, this decision has contributed to a boost in market confidence, leading to positive movements in various financial sectors. The extension of the ceasefire is seen as a stabilizing factor, alleviating some geopolitical tensions that have been influencing market dynamics.

The cryptocurrency market, often sensitive to geopolitical developments, reacted positively to the news, with Bitcoin leading the charge in terms of price appreciation. This upward trend in Bitcoin's value reflects a broader sentiment of optimism among investors, who are hopeful that the extension will lead to more stable conditions in the region. The equities market mirrored this sentiment, with stocks also showing gains as investors responded to the news with increased buying activity.

Market analysts suggest that the extension of the ceasefire could have a lasting impact on investor sentiment, potentially leading to sustained growth in both cryptocurrency and traditional markets. The announcement has provided a temporary reprieve from the uncertainties that have been affecting global markets, offering a window of opportunity for investors to capitalize on the positive momentum. As the situation develops, market participants will be closely monitoring any further announcements or changes in the geopolitical landscape that could influence future market movements.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 78,000 USDT with a 2.81% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 22, 2026, 05:22 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 78,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 78,073.539063 USDT, with a narrowed 2.81% increase in 24 hours.
